name: Eiffel Tower
clue: An iron lattice giant built as a temporary exhibit in 1889, it was almost torn down but saved by becoming a radio transmission tower.

name: Great Wall of China
clue: Stretching over 13,000 miles, this ancient fortification was built to keep out northern nomadic invaders and took centuries to complete.

name: Machu Picchu
clue: Perched 2,430 meters above sea level in the Andes, this 15th-century citadel was unknown to the outside world until 1911.

name: Mount Everest
clue: The highest point on Earth above sea level, it sits on a border between two nations and was first summited in 1953.

name: Colosseum
clue: This 2,000-year-old elliptical amphitheatre once hosted gladiatorial contests and could seat up to 80,000 spectators.

name: Statue of Liberty
clue: A gift from France dedicated in 1886, this copper lady holding a torch stands on a small island in a famous harbor.

name: Sydney Opera House
clue: Its distinctive shell-shaped roof was inspired by orange segments, and it took 14 years to build on a harbor promontory.

name: Sahara Desert
clue: The world's largest hot desert spans 11 countries and was once a lush savanna just 6,000 years ago.

name: Angkor Wat
clue: The world's largest religious monument was built in the 12th century and appears on its nation's flag.

name: Victoria Falls
clue: Known locally as 'The Smoke That Thunders,' this waterfall is twice as wide and tall as Niagara and sits on a border between two African nations.

name: Petra
clue: An ancient city carved entirely into rose-red sandstone cliffs, it was the capital of a Nabataean kingdom and lost to the Western world for centuries.

name: Bora Bora
clue: A dormant volcanic island surrounded by a turquoise lagoon and coral reef in the South Pacific, it served as a US military base in WWII.

name: Amazon River Delta
clue: The mouth of the world's largest river by discharge empties into the Atlantic Ocean and releases so much freshwater it can be detected 100 miles offshore.

name: Serengeti National Park
clue: Every year, over 1.5 million wildebeest migrate in a circular pattern across this vast East African ecosystem in what is called the Greatest Show on Earth.

name: Geysir, Iceland
clue: This geothermal field gave its name to all erupting hot springs worldwide. The island it sits on straddles two tectonic plates.

name: Cappadocia
clue: Famous for its fairy chimneys, underground cities, and hot air balloon rides at sunrise over a surreal volcanic landscape.

name: Bagan
clue: A plain dotted with over 2,000 ancient Buddhist temples and pagodas, built between the 9th and 13th centuries in Southeast Asia.

name: Torres del Paine
clue: Three iconic granite towers rise dramatically from a windswept plateau at the southern tip of South America, a trekker's paradise.

name: Dead Sea
clue: The lowest point on Earth's surface, its extreme salinity means nothing lives in it and every swimmer floats effortlessly.

name: Socotra Island
clue: Called the 'Galápagos of the Indian Ocean,' this remote archipelago is home to the alien-looking Dragon Blood Tree found nowhere else on Earth.

name: Lake Baikal
clue: The world's deepest lake holds 20% of all unfrozen surface freshwater on Earth and is home to the world's only freshwater seal species.

name: Salar de Uyuni
clue: The world's largest salt flat sits at 3,656 meters elevation and becomes a giant mirror reflecting the sky after rainfall, used to calibrate satellites.

name: Tsingy de Bemaraha
clue: A UNESCO World Heritage Site of razor-sharp limestone pinnacles rising from a plateau on the world's fourth-largest island, inaccessible for much of the year.

name: Ellesmere Island
clue: The world's tenth-largest island sits in the high Arctic, home to ancient ice shelves, musk oxen, and a lake with a permanently frozen surface.

name: Dallol, Ethiopia
clue: The hottest year-round inhabited place on Earth sits below sea level in a volcanic depression, featuring alien acid ponds of yellow, green, and red.
